messages.text: Molding Line 1 Mid Shift.
Woohoo great midshift 🥰
A water test was performed on 5 chocolate bars, and 3 have air leaks. The line leader was notified.
Glycol is good
Metal Detector belt and C6 belt are running correctly
Great production!
Start of shift checklist
Countdown. Nathan Gevenois

Organoleptic test check update ✅️ 


The product's appearance looks good; its odor and texture are acceptable, falling within the permissible ranges and limits required by the product specifications.

Tempering chart test check update ✅️ 


The temperature is very stable and has remained within the acceptable range required by the Centerline.

Weight test check update ✅️ 


The weight looks good; we are within the parameters required by the product specifications.

Packaging flow-wrap checking update 


 


Upon inspection of the packaging and flow wrap, the configuration is found to be 24 units per display and 12 displays per case; the flow wrap features high-quality printing, and the "Best If Used By" date is legible and clearly visible in the location required by product specifications.
However, by checking the vacuum test with the air pressure machine, 10-piece test 4 I got a small leak at the ends of the corners of the product.
Molds appear to be good 👍
Molds appear good
Thank you
We finished the 30742 - Case12 order, we are now changing over to Bulk216 pieces.
Molds appear to be good
The last check on this page I wrote the slope and mass cooling temp reversed

Organoleptic test check update 


The product's appearance looks good; its odor and texture are acceptable, falling within the permissible ranges and limits required by the product specifications.

Weight test check update 


The weight looks good; we are within the parameters required by the product specifications.

Tempering chart test check update 


The temperature is very stable and has remained within the acceptable range required by the Centerline.
Molds appear to be good

Packaging flow-wrap check update 


 


Upon inspection of the packaging and flow wrap, the configuration is found to be 216 units per box the flow wrap features high-quality printing, and the "Best If Used By" date is legible and clearly visible in the location required by product specifications.
Glycol is good
Metal detector belt and C6 belt are running correctly

Line verification and start of production (3rd shift): part weight, organoleptic properties, part thickness, temper, flow-wrap specifications, case weight, final case label, pallet label, and water test—all within the correct ranges.
C6 Belt running correctly,  MD belt running correctly
Verification and count of a final box: weight 16.75, 216 pieces—correct.
Batch change CC26200I1, Date 18/MAR/28 correct
Coolers have been opened to verify molds aren’t damaged, just grease detected. Line is back up
Stopped depositing at 1:45am, running out coolers
Started at 2:45 level 2 clean
ATP CORRECT SWECO SCREEN AND MAGNET,
Passed visual section deposit and vibration
It is 5:30, and no visual check has been performed on the conveyor belt section or the line cabin.
Passed visual cabana
ATP FILTER PTL CORRECT
All the bands underwent a visual inspection for assembly, were run for 5 minutes to verify proper setup, and were washed with water to prepare for the ATP test.
ATP DEMOLDING CORRECT
ATP METAL DETECTOR CORRECT
Product date change
The sensor that read the product is failing, maintenance is working on it
This got fixed at 8:35am, the line is running.
Will do. Not excusing it, 5s staging with signs will definitely help prevent this from recurring
MFM Mid shift.
Change date

Good morning, please it is requested to load the system the wip labels are using on the line, there are several pallets that are physically consumed and not loaded to the system, not doing so delays us in the elaboration of label and discrepancies in the system, today we have found 3 pallets that have already been consumed and not loaded, I can not determine which shift consumed, Rich Brierley Marcos Montano Alan Greer
We have 1 full pallet and a partial left of WIP before changing over to Caramel cups.
Good afternoon, we are having problems with the printing of date tickets, we are reviewing solved the necessary labes will be printed Itzel Chavez
Lot change
Done, the printer is ok
WIP for Sea salt caramels is finished, we are doing the line clearance to perform the changeover to caramel cups.
Set up is ready
Change over: post operational and pre operational its done✅
As we were running the lead found 2 pieces of caramel twist wrap. We stopped to empty it out and do another inspection

A product different from the two scheduled for production was detected on the MFM line. As a corrective 





action, the matrix was emptied, the product contained within it was verified, and the upstream areas were re-checked to ensure compliance with the process.
Starting level 2 clean
Operator has clarified that this pallet was staged there since start of shift. She continued running 4/13, then 4/14.
Level 2 clean complete along with set up for assorted.
Level 2 final cleaning and correct ATP at the MFM
Enrober tempering chart - SoS.
Enrober CL - SoS.
Caramel and Bed CL - SoS.

Weight = 876


Pieces = 44
Weight checks - 8:00am.
Weight checks - 9:00am.
Viscosity, WA, Brix
Weight checks - 11:00am.
Belt inspection sheet - Midshift.
Weight checks - 12:00pm.

There is no chocolate left in WH (10226), they are waiting for the driver to pick it up from L3, but he is currently in SV. The ETA is 1 hour and 30 minutes.
We only have 15 boxes left of chocolate, MT percentage will most likely go as low as 40% - 50% (which can result in temperature issues), depending on how long it takes for the driver to get here.
Just a heads up. Justin, Nathan, Jazmin.
If temperature issues occur with the MT, I will have to slow down the line to allow the MT's temperature to recover. Nathan Topham
Thank you for catching this S3 Enrober (Jesus)
Tiffanee and i are still working on this Justin we are figuring out how many more we need to last 2nd and 3rd shift and have tanks full on Monday
Weight checks - 13:00pm.
Justin due to the timing of the chocolate coming we will slow down the line speed to 60rr.  In another 30 minutes we will evaluate again how much chcoclate we have used and adjust again from there.

As per chocolate matrix we are ok to mix 10226 to 10200.  We will get with Paula to make a new work order so we can start melting right away Justin.  There is still 10226 chocolate on its way, 12 pallets.
We are creating a new work order with item 10200 instead of 10226. IMS Paula is creating it and will post it here shortly. Nathan Topham
Here is the new WO for the chocolate change. Nathan Topham, William Beattie, Luis Falcon, S3 Enrober
Weight checks - 14:00pm.

We would still need to keep the line speed at 60 to avoid triggering out the MT motor due to low temperature and cause an even bigger issue. The MT level is at 40% as of 2:05pm, which means I will need to melt chocolate slow and steady for about 30 -60 minutes. Nathan Topham

I believe we will need to do a reheat. The riser pump is not starting, and I am getting an alarm that reads "high chocolate pressure at turbotemper intlet."


The return pump is working just fine with a bar pressure of 2.4 when set to manual. I also inspected the Russell finex pin and is intact. Before this issue, the riser pump bar pressure was reading 4.5, and there is a lot of visible build up in the enrober. 


I will get maintenance involved, but I still believe a reheat is required.
Justin, Nathan

I evaluated the situation with maintenance and decided to do a reheat to avoid damaging the equipment since there is a lot of build-up  I the Enrober and also could not find any other reason for the riser pump not starting other than high bar pressure. I will get with Nathan Topham  to decide if a full reheat is needed or just a partial reheat.
We will do a full reheat. The last reheat that was done was on Thursday 1st shift Justin. This reheat will help prevent the same issue that happened on sol 2.
Belt inspection sheet - EoS.
We are purging the system then going to put it in program
I had my team open the cover for the screw drive and  there was about 30lbs of cold chocolate build up
This is some of the nuts we pulled out of the wire belt and tensioning roller
Thank you
Thank you
No problem!
We are also putting it In program
Excellent!!
I’m sending my team to while  we wait for temper
The line has started
Pin intact
MD rollers

We’re stopping to do a pause to pass accumulation in the pack area. 

Will message back when we start up the line.
Line has started again.
Pin intact
Enrober EoS

We did a brief 15-minute pause to clean the Tunnel 2 scraper and the cross conveyor scraper CILT. The line has started back up Justin
Can we work on bringing the almond weight down please.
we are working on thinning out the bedding.  Next post should reflect better Justin.
Great work bringing down the almond weights! Thank you!
